codeReview.summary: The repository cleanup and sensitive-crypto regressions are implemented and well-covered, but the feature is incomplete because ensure_root_user lacks the requested focused runtime regression and the feature commit bundles many unrelated files beyond this runtime migration slice.

issues.description: The feature contract explicitly requires a focused runtime regression for ensure_root_user, but the tree only contains a helper-shape unit test for root_user and no runtime test that exercises signin-first/signup-fallback behavior or the combined error context when both calls fail.

The reviewed commit 64d4657 includes broad unrelated mission/library/validation/macro files in addition to the runtime-sensitive code paths, so this feature handoff does not isolate its claimed scope and makes review/audit harder.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-edition-upgrade-worker skill says to add focused runtime regressions for the assigned behavior, but it does not give a concrete fallback for auth-path coverage when no dedicated ensure_root_user seam exists, and this review found that gap persisted into the delivered feature.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature handoff coverage note says no dedicated ensure_root_user runtime test existed; skillFeedback.suggestedChanges also requests a fallback procedure, while core/tests contains no ensure_root_user test match.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library notes capture repository and crypto migration hotspots but do not record how auth bootstrap behavior should be regression-tested under Rust 2024, which likely contributed to the missing ensure_root_user coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: C:/Users/admin/appdb/.factory/library/rust-2024-upgrade.md lists core/src/auth/mod.rs as a hotspot, but no shared library note describes an auth regression seam or verification method for ensure_root_user.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit 64d4657. Repository cleanup, duplicate-batch protection, encrypted-at-rest, single-flight init, and auto-init failure coverage look correct, but the feature still misses the requested ensure_root_user runtime regression, so I marked the review as fail.
